lede: A look at November 20 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Timothy Kitum and Rodger Bumpass, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: North Macedonia
events.desc: North Macedonia's deadliest aviation disaster occurs when Avioimpex Flight 110, a Yakovlev Yak-42, crashes near Ohrid Airport, killing all 116 people on board.

events.title: Andrei Chikatilo
events.desc: Andrei Chikatilo, one of the Soviet Union's most prolific serial killers, is arrested; he eventually confesses to 56 killings.

events.title: Grand Mosque seizure
events.desc: Grand Mosque seizure: About 200 Sunni Muslims revolt in Saudi Arabia at the site of the Kaaba in Mecca during the pilgrimage and take about 6,000 hostages. The Saudi government receives help from French special forces to put down the uprising.

events.title: Egypt
events.desc: Egyptian President Anwar Sadat becomes the first Arab leader to officially visit Israel, when he meets Israeli prime minister Menachem Begin and speaks before the Knesset in Jerusalem, seeking a permanent peace settlement.

events.title: Boeing 747
events.desc: The first fatal crash of a Boeing 747 occurs when Lufthansa Flight 540 crashes while attempting to takeoff from Jomo Kenyatta International Airport in Nairobi, Kenya, killing 59 out of the 157 people on board.

events.title: Elizabeth II
events.desc: The Princess Elizabeth (the future Queen) marries Lieutenant Philip Mountbatten, who becomes the Duke of Edinburgh, at Westminster Abbey in London.

events.title: Nuremberg trials
events.desc: Nuremberg trials: Trials against 24 Nazi war criminals start at the Palace of Justice at Nuremberg.

events.title: World War I
events.desc: World War I: Battle of Cambrai begins: British forces make early progress in an attack on German positions but are later pushed back.

events.title: American Revolutionary War
events.desc: American Revolutionary War: British forces land at the Palisades and then attack Fort Lee. The Continental Army starts to retreat across New Jersey.

events.title: Diocletian
events.desc: Diocletian is chosen as Roman emperor.

births.name: Pope Pius VIII
births.line: Head of the Catholic Church from 1829 to 1830

births.name: Joe Biden
births.line: American politician, 46th President of the United States

births.name: Robert F. Kennedy
births.line: US Navy officer, lawyer, and politician, 64th United States Attorney General

deaths.text: John McEwen, Australian lawyer and politician, 18th Prime Minister of Australia
Francisco Franco, Spanish general and dictator, Prime Minister of Spain
John I of France, king of France and Navarra

observances.text: Christian feast day: Blessed Anacleto González Flores, José Sánchez del Río, and companions (Martyrs of Cristero War)
